items.title: SQLite in production
items.impact: Cannot scale beyond single instance. 56 .db files with no shared schema.
items.fix: Migrate to PostgreSQL with async driver (asyncpg). Consolidate into 3-5 databases.

items.title: Brain Pipeline runs synchronously
items.impact: LLM calls (Step 9) and pentest (Step 10) block the request thread for 10-60s.
items.fix: Convert to async pipeline with asyncio.gather() for independent steps.

items.title: Brain Pipeline _runs dict was unbounded (FIXED 2026-03-02)
items.impact: Memory leak — _runs dict grew without bound. Now capped at MAX_RUNS_HISTORY=1000.
items.fix: FIXED: Added eviction logic at brain_pipeline.py:247-253.

items.title: 101 bare except:pass patterns (Bandit B110)
items.impact: Silently swallows exceptions, making debugging very difficult. Worst offenders: playbook_runner.py (19), single_agent.py (16), dast_engine.py (16), mcp_server.py (10).
items.fix: Replace bare except:pass with specific exception handling and logging.

items.title: 27 potential SQL injection vectors (Bandit B608)
items.impact: String-based SQL query construction detected by bandit. Most are false positives (parameterized WHERE clauses use f-string only for column names, not user values). Verified safe: exposure_case.py uses ? placeholders for all user-supplied values.
items.fix: Audit remaining 27 locations. Refactor to avoid f-string SQL entirely where feasible.

items.title: 77 ruff lint warnings (all E402 architectural)
items.impact: Code style: 77 E402 (module-import-not-at-top). All caused by sitecustomize.py pattern. Zero actionable issues.
items.fix: E402 is architectural (sitecustomize.py pattern). All F401/F821 fixed. 0 actionable items.
items.note: Run 7: Fixed 2 F821 (undefined-name Event) in eventbus_integration.py via TYPE_CHECKING import. 79→77 total. All zero actionable.

items.title: app.py is 2,742 LOC with 34 router mounts
items.impact: Single file is hard to maintain and causes slow startup as all routers load.
items.fix: Split into router groups (core_routers.py, attack_routers.py, etc.) loaded by category.

items.title: No database migration system
items.impact: Schema changes require manual DB recreation. No rollback capability.
items.fix: Implement Alembic migrations for all SQLite databases. Required before PostgreSQL migration.

items.title: Test coverage at 4.99% (gate: 25%)
items.impact: CI gate fails. pyproject.toml config was fixed (DEMO-006) but coverage includes all modules now, many at 0%.
items.fix: Focus test writing on measured modules. Target: 25% minimum across all suites.
items.note: Coverage dropped from 19% because pyproject.toml now correctly measures ALL suites. Latest verified run: 4.99% (288 core tests).

items.title: 15 file:/ URL audit warnings (Bandit B310)
items.impact: url_open calls accept file:// scheme, potential LFI if user-controlled.
items.fix: Add URL scheme validation to reject file:// and other dangerous schemes.

items.title: In-process EventBus (no external message queue)
items.impact: Events lost on restart. Cannot distribute events across multiple processes.
items.fix: Add Redis Pub/Sub or RabbitMQ for production event distribution.

items.title: SAST engine is regex-based (16 re.search rules)
items.impact: High false positive rate. Cannot understand code flow or data flow.
items.fix: Phase 3: Add AST-based analysis using tree-sitter for Python/JS. Regex stays as fast pre-filter.

items.title: 26 subprocess calls without shell=False verification (Bandit B603/B607)
items.impact: subprocess calls with potentially untrusted input could allow command injection.
items.fix: Audit all subprocess calls, ensure shell=False and input sanitization.

items.title: Scanner ingest stats are in-memory only
items.impact: Ingestion statistics reset on process restart.
items.fix: Persist stats to SQLite alongside scanner_ingest operations.

items.title: XML parser vulnerability (B314/B405) — FIXED 2026-03-02
items.impact: scanner_parsers.py used xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring which is vulnerable to XXE attacks.
items.fix: FIXED: Added defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() at module load + defusedxml.ElementTree.fromstring as primary parser. Regex DOCTYPE/ENTITY stripping as fallback.

items.title: CORS allows wildcard *.devinapps.com in default config
items.impact: If FIXOPS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not set, CORS allows requests from any *.devinapps.com subdomain. Safe for dev, risk for production.
items.fix: Remove *.devinapps.com from default origins. Require explicit FIXOPS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS in production mode.

items.title: SQLite connection leaks in history.py — FIXED 2026-03-02
items.impact: 5 methods in history.py create sqlite3.connect() without try/finally. FIXED.
items.fix: FIXED: Wrapped all 5 sqlite3.connect() blocks in try/finally: conn.close().

items.title: No circuit breakers for LLM and MPTE calls in Brain Pipeline
items.impact: When LLM API or MPTE service is down, every Brain Pipeline run wastes 60-120s per call on timeouts before fallback. With circuit breaker, subsequent calls would short-circuit in <1ms after 3 failures.
items.fix: Reuse _AsyncCircuitBreaker from suite-core/connectors/universal_connector.py. Add module-level circuit breakers for LLM (Step 9) and MPTE (Step 10). State: OPEN after 3 failures, recovery after 60s.

items.title: No per-step timeouts in Brain Pipeline (Steps 3,5,6,7,8,11)
items.impact: Individual steps can hang without detection until the 300s global pipeline timeout. Steps 9 (LLM) and 10 (MPTE) already have thread-pool timeouts. Steps 3,5,6,7,8,11 do not.
items.fix: Add per-step configurable timeouts: 30s default, 60s LLM, 120s MPTE. Use threading.Timer or signal.alarm for enforcement.

items.title: Dedup Service opens N DB connections per batch
items.impact: process_finding() opens a new sqlite3.connect() for EACH finding. For 1000 findings = 1000 connections opened/closed. SQLite open/close is ~1ms per connection. At 10K findings = ~10s overhead from connection management alone.
items.fix: Refactor process_findings_batch() to open ONE connection, pass to process_finding(), batch commit every 100 findings.

items.title: Brain Pipeline Steps 9+10 sequential but independent
items.impact: LLM consensus (Step 9, up to 60s) and MPTE (Step 10, up to 120s) run sequentially. Both are I/O-bound and independent. Combined worst case: 180s. If parallelized: max(60s, 120s) = 120s. Saves 60s per pipeline run.
items.fix: Use asyncio.gather() or concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor to run Steps 9 and 10 in parallel when both are enabled.

items.title: AutoFixEngine created per finding in Step 11 — FIXED 2026-03-02
items.impact: AutoFixEngine() was instantiated inside the per-finding loop in Step 11 (run_playbooks). Each instance creates empty caches. For 50 blocked findings = 50 instantiations.
items.fix: FIXED: Hoisted AutoFixEngine creation outside the loop. Now O(1) instantiation.

items.title: AutoFix _fixes dict was unbounded — FIXED 2026-03-03
items.impact: AutoFixEngine._fixes dict grew without bound. Each fix is 5-50KB. At 10K fixes = ~500MB memory. Long-running processes would OOM.
items.fix: FIXED: Added MAX_FIXES_STORED=5000 with eviction of oldest entries. autofix_engine.py lines 230-231 (constants) and 406-415 (eviction logic).

items.title: No prompt injection protection in AutoFix LLM calls
items.impact: Finding title, description, and source code are injected directly into LLM prompt via f-string. Malicious finding data could manipulate LLM behavior. MITIGATED by 7-point safety gate on output.
items.fix: Use structured prompt templates or content escaping. Defense-in-depth: safety gate catches dangerous outputs.

items.title: AutoFix _history list was unbounded — FIXED 2026-03-03
items.impact: AutoFixEngine._history list grew without bound. At 100K actions, ~50MB memory. get_history(limit) only sliced on read.
items.fix: FIXED: Added MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES=10000 with tail eviction. autofix_engine.py lines 230-231 (constants) and 424-425 (eviction logic).

items.title: BulkGenerateRequest.findings list unbounded at Pydantic level
items.impact: BulkGenerateRequest accepts unlimited findings list. Router slices at 20 but full payload is parsed first. Large payloads cause memory spike.
items.fix: Add max_items=100 to Pydantic Field definition in autofix_router.py.

items.title: MCP tools/call bypasses FastAPI auth (direct handler invocation)
items.impact: MCPProtocolHandler._handle_tools_call directly invokes endpoint functions stored in tool.handler, bypassing FastAPI's Depends(_verify_api_key) dependency injection. MCP clients can execute tools without per-endpoint auth checks.
items.fix: Add explicit auth verification in _handle_tools_call before invoking handler, or route through ASGI stack to preserve middleware chain.

items.title: TestClient instantiation per /mcp/execute request
items.impact: mcp_router.py /execute endpoint creates TestClient(request.app) for every call. TestClient creates a full ASGI test environment (threading, event loop). Extremely expensive for high-traffic use.
items.fix: Replace with direct ASGI routing via app.router.handle() or httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport.

items.title: MCP protocol router broken attribute access — FIXED 2026-03-03
items.impact: All 10 /api/v1/mcp-protocol/* endpoints were broken: handler.sessions.sessions, handler.tools.tools, etc. accessed non-existent attributes, causing immediate AttributeError on every request.
items.fix: FIXED: All endpoints now use get_mcp_handler() singleton and correct paths (session_manager, tool_registry, resource_server, prompt_library).

items.title: MCP audit log should use collections.deque
items.impact: Audit log uses list slicing for eviction (_audit_log = _audit_log[-5000:]) which creates a new 5K-element list. deque(maxlen=10000) would give O(1) eviction.
items.fix: Replace self._audit_log: List[Dict] with collections.deque(maxlen=10000).

items.title: MCP SSE stream uses blocking time.sleep(30)
items.impact: sse_stream() in mcp_server.py uses time.sleep(30) for keepalive. This is a synchronous blocking call that will block the event loop thread in async FastAPI.
items.fix: Convert sse_stream to async generator using asyncio.sleep(30). Update StreamingResponse to use async generator.

items.title: MCP protocol version inconsistency
items.impact: mcp_server.py says PROTOCOL_VERSION='2025-03-26' while mcp_router.py says mcp_version='2024-11-05'. Confusing for clients parsing version info.
items.fix: Unify on '2025-03-26' (the Protocol Engine version). Update MCPCatalogStats and MCPHealthResponse defaults.

items.title: Self-learning /stats endpoint was missing — FIXED 2026-03-03
items.impact: GET /api/v1/self-learning/stats returned 404. Was the only endpoint in the sprint burndown still returning non-200.
items.fix: FIXED: Added /stats endpoint returning aggregated feedback loop statistics, weight summaries, and config.

items.title: FAIL Engine _history list was unbounded — FIXED 2026-03-07
items.impact: FAILEngine._history grew without bound. Each FAILResult is ~2-5KB. At 100K scores = ~500MB. Long-running processes would OOM.
items.fix: FIXED: Added MAX_HISTORY_SIZE=5000 with eviction logic in score() method. fail_engine.py lines 290-291 (constant) and 350-352 (eviction).

items.title: FAIL scoring not integrated with Brain Pipeline Step 7
items.impact: Two independent risk scoring systems: Brain Pipeline uses simple deterministic/ML formula, FAIL Engine uses 4-dimensional evidence-based scoring. No integration point. Users see different risk scores depending on which path is queried.
items.fix: Brain Pipeline Step 7 should optionally call FAILEngine.score() as enrichment. Store fail_score alongside risk_score on findings.

items.title: FAIL batch scoring silently drops failed items
items.impact: POST /api/v1/fail/score/batch swallows exceptions for individual items. Response shows total = successful only. Caller cannot identify which items failed or why.
items.fix: Return partial results + error entries: {results: [...], errors: [{index, error}]}.

items.title: FAIL /delete endpoint lacks org_id authorization check
items.impact: DELETE /api/v1/fail/score/{score_id} has no Depends(get_org_id) — any caller can delete any score. Other FAIL endpoints properly use get_org_id.
items.fix: Add org_id: str = Depends(get_org_id) and verify score belongs to org before deletion.

items.title: ExposureCaseManager singleton ignores db_path on subsequent calls
items.impact: First call to get_instance(db_path) locks the path. Subsequent calls with different db_path silently return existing instance with original path.
items.fix: Assert db_path matches existing instance, or use dict-keyed-by-path pattern.

summary.ruff_note: Run 10: Fixed 1 F401 (trend_analyzer.py) + 1 F841 (micro_pentest_router.py). Now 77 E402 architectural only.
summary.test_coverage_note: Run 10 measured. Coverage gate 25%. pyproject.toml now measures ALL suites, so percentage dropped. 237 core tests + 138 FAIL tests + 73 self-learning = 448 tests verified this run.
summary.reliability_review: 2026-03-02 — Grade B-. History.py connection leaks FIXED (TD-017). AutoFixEngine per-finding FIXED (TD-022). Dedup connection-per-finding logged (TD-020).
summary.performance_review: 2026-03-02 Run 7 — Grade B. Dedup N-connections (TD-020). Steps 9+10 sequential (TD-021). AutoFixEngine loop FIXED (TD-022). All memory bounds enforced. Parallelization blueprint documented.
summary.autofix_review: 2026-03-03 Run 8 — Grade B+. 13 endpoints, 10 fix types, 7-point safety gate, ML confidence. _fixes unbounded FIXED (TD-023). _history unbounded FIXED (TD-025). Prompt injection logged (TD-024).
summary.mcp_review: 2026-03-03 Run 9 — Grade B-. 9 broken attribute accesses FIXED (TD-029). Auth bypass logged (TD-027). TestClient per-request logged (TD-028). SSE blocking logged (TD-031). Stats endpoints added.
summary.fail_review: 2026-03-07 Run 10 — Grade B+. 4-dimensional scoring, dynamic weights, memory fix. FAIL not integrated with Brain Pipeline (TD-035). Delete lacks auth (TD-037).
bugs_fixed_run7: F821 undefined-name Event in eventbus_integration.py (TYPE_CHECKING import)
bugs_fixed_run9: TD-029 (MCP protocol router 9 broken attribute accesses → singleton + correct paths)
bugs_fixed_run10: F841 unused variable in micro_pentest_router.py (now included in health response)
summary.adrs_validated: 11/11 (Run 10: ADR-011 added for FAIL Engine scoring)
